a: Transfer data between a watchOS app and its companion iOS app.
A structure that contains Watch Connectivity error information.
Constants for errors during a session.
An error indicating that the companion hasn’t installed the app.
An error that occurs when the system can’t deliver the payload.
An error indicating that the current device doesn’t have a paired counterpart.
An error indicating that the system can’t transfer a file because it is inaccessible.
An error that occurs when there is an unknown problem.
An error indicating that there isn’t enough space on the receiving side to store the data.
An error indicating that a parameter is invalid.
An error that occurs when the system can’t return the reply.
An error that occurs when the counterpart app doesn’t return a reply in time.
An error indicating that the counterpart app isn’t reachable.
An error indicating an attempt to send an item that exceeds the maximum size limit.
An error indicating that a dictionary contains nonproperty list types.
An error indicating that the session is inactive.
An error indicating that the WatchKit extension doesn’t have a valid extension delegate to process events.
An error indicating that the other device doesn’t have an active session.
An error indicating that the current device doesn’t support the use of session objects.
An error that occurs when the transfer reaches the timeout limit before it completes.
An error indicating that the Watch app isn’t an installed app on the user’s Apple Watch.
An error indicating that the app is a watch-only app.
The domain for errors associated with the Watch Connectivity framework.
The object that initiates communication between a WatchKit extension and its companion iOS app.
Constants indicating the activation state of a session.
The session is active and the Watch app and iOS app may communicate with each other freely.

The session was active but is transitioning to the deactivated state. The session’s delegate object may still receive data while in this state, but it is a programmer error to try to send data to the counterpart app.
The session is not activated. When in this state, no communication occurs between the Watch app and iOS app. It is a programmer error to try to send data to the counterpart app while in this state.
A delegate protocol that defines methods for receiving messages sent by a [WCSession](/documentation/watchconnectivity/wcsession) object.
Information about a file currently being transferred between an iOS app and WatchKit extension.
Information about in-progress file transfers.
Information about in-progress data transfers.
Implement two-way communication between an iOS app and its paired watchOS app.
Activates the session asynchronously.
The current activation state of the session.
The most recent contextual data sent to the paired and active device.
Cancels the data transfer.
Cancels the file transfer.
An error indicating that the companion hasn’t installed the app.
Returns the singleton session object for the current device.
The delegate for the session object
An error that occurs when the system can’t deliver the payload.
An error indicating that the current device doesn’t have a paired counterpart.
The file being transferred.
An error indicating that the system can’t transfer a file because it is inaccessible.
The URL of the file that was received.
An error that occurs when there is an unknown problem.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the session has more content to deliver.
A Boolean value indicating whether the paired iPhone must be in an unlocked state to be reachable.
An error indicating that there isn’t enough space on the receiving side to store the data.
An error indicating that a parameter is invalid.
A Boolean value indicating whether the companion has installed the app.
A Boolean value indicating whether the Watch app’s complication is in use on the currently paired and active Apple Watch.
A Boolean indicating whether the data is related to the app’s complication.
A Boolean indicating whether the current iPhone has a paired Apple Watch.
A Boolean value indicating whether the counterpart app is available for live messaging.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the current iOS device is able to use a session object.
A Boolean value indicating whether the data is still being transferred.
A Boolean value indicating whether the file is still being transferred.
A Boolean value indicating whether the currently paired and active Apple Watch has installed the app.
An error that occurs when the system can’t return the reply.
An error that occurs when the counterpart app doesn’t return a reply in time.
A dictionary of additional information that was sent with the file.
An error indicating that the counterpart app isn’t reachable.
An array of in-progress file transfers.
An array of in-progress data transfers.
An error indicating an attempt to send an item that exceeds the maximum size limit.
An error indicating that a dictionary contains nonproperty list types.
An object that tracks the progress of the file transfer.
A dictionary containing the last update data received from a paired and active device.
The number of remaining times you can send complication data from the iOS app to the WatchKit extension.
Sends a message immediately to the paired and active device and optionally handles a response.
Sends a data object immediately to the paired and active device and optionally handles a response.
Tells the delegate that the session has finished activating.
Tells the delegate that a file transfer has finished successfully or ended because of an error.
Tells the delegate that a data transfer operation has finished successfully or ended because of an error.
Tells the delegate that the session successfully received a file from its counterpart.
Tells the delegate that the session has received context data from the counterpart.
Tells the delegate that an immediate message has arrived.
Tells the delegate that an immediate message has arrived, and it requires a response.
Tells the delegate that an immediate data message has arrived.
Tells the delegate that an immediate data message has arrived, and it requires a response.
Tells the delegate that the session successfully received a data directory from its counterpart.
Indicates a change to the companion app’s installed state.
Tells the delegate that the session will stop communicating with the current Apple Watch.
Tells the delegate that the session has delivered all the data from the previous session, and that communication with the Apple Watch has ended.
An error indicating that the session is inactive.
An error indicating that the WatchKit extension doesn’t have a valid extension delegate to process events.
An error indicating that the other device doesn’t have an active session.
An error indicating that the current device doesn’t support the use of session objects.
Indicates a change to the counterpart’s reachability status.
Indicates a change to the counterpart’s information.
Sends complication-related data from the iOS app to the WatchKit extension.
Sends the specified file and optional dictionary to the counterpart.
An error that occurs when the transfer reaches the timeout limit before it completes.
Sends the specified data dictionary to the counterpart.
Sends a dictionary of values that a paired and active device can use to synchronize its state.
The data being transferred.
An error indicating that the Watch app isn’t an installed app on the user’s Apple Watch.
A directory for storing information specific to the currently paired and active Apple Watch.
An error indicating that the app is a watch-only app.
